# Surfaced — Product Marketing Context

*Generated by /build-brand. Pre-fills downstream marketing skills (marketing-plan, content, launch-strategy) so they skip redundant intake questions.*

---

## Product Overview
- **Brand name:** Surfaced
- **Category:** AI-powered outbound sales pipeline (B2B SaaS)
- **Subcategory:** Pain-signal-first voice outreach for AI service founders
- **Stage:** Hackathon MVP → early product
- **Core mechanism:** Real-time pain signal detection from public data → personalized call script → VAPI voice call. Config to completed call in under 3 minutes.

---

## Budget & Constraints
- **Budget tier:** Bootstrapped / low (<$1K/mo)
- **Key constraint / unfair advantage:** Real-time pain signal detection from public reviews and websites before any contact is made. This is the product differentiation, not just a feature.
- **Existing audience:** None. Distribution strategy TBD — fill during marketing-plan intake.

---

## Brand Essence
- **Purpose:** The cold call is broken because it starts with a pitch, not a problem. Surfaced exists to find the evidence first.
- **Mission:** Surface the specific, public evidence that a business is struggling — then put that evidence in the opening line of every call.
- **Brand essence:** "Find the wound. Open with the receipt."
- **Brand pillars:** Evidence · Specificity · Speed

---

## Target Audience

**Demographics:**
- Founders of AI service companies (voice agents, automation tools, chatbots)
- Company size: 1–10 people
- Revenue stage: pre-revenue to ~$500K ARR
- Primarily US-based, English-speaking

**Psychographics:**
- Builder mindset — ships before decks, demos before docs
- Sceptical of enterprise bloat and generic outbound tools
- Believes AI can automate almost anything, including the thing they haven't automated yet (their own sales)
- Identity: "I build things that automate other people's problems" — the irony is they're doing outbound manually

**ICP — Jobs-to-Be-Done (JTBD format):**
"When I've built an AI service I believe in but have no consistent way to find buyers, I want to reach prospects who already have the specific problem I solve — so I can open with evidence, not a pitch."

**Customer pain phrases (in their language):**
- "I know exactly who should be buying this. I just have no way to reach them at scale without it feeling gross."
- "Every outbound tool I've tried just blasts emails that get ignored."
- "Our best clients came from warm intros. I can't build a company on that."
- "I need to be building the product, not spending a Sunday researching booking pages."

**Where they are:**
- Twitter/X — AI builder community, VAPI/Retell/n8n circles
- Product Hunt, Indie Hackers
- YC / alumni networks
- Discord servers for AI builders and automation founders
- LinkedIn (ironically, doing manual outreach themselves)

---

## Competitive Landscape

**Primary alternatives:**
- **Clay** — closest philosophically (research-driven personalisation) but requires building the pipeline yourself; no voice; no live signal detection
- **Apollo** — large dataset, intent signals, but personalisation = merge fields; built for SDR teams not founders
- **11x / Artisan** — autonomous AI SDR but enterprise-priced ($1.5K–$5K/mo) and requires a sales team to manage
- **Instantly / Smartlead** — email volume tools; no research, no voice, no signal detection

**White space Surfaced owns:**
- Real-time pain signal detection (not historical data, not enrichment fields — live public signals)
- Voice as the primary channel (not email-first with voice tacked on)
- Built for the 1–10 person AI founder, not an enterprise SDR team
- No list import, no sequences, no setup — two fields and run

---

## Positioning

**Positioning statement (Dunford format):**
"For AI service founders who need outbound that converts, Surfaced is the outreach pipeline that opens every call with verifiable evidence of the prospect's pain — unlike Clay or Apollo, where personalisation means merge fields, not proof."

**Value proposition:**
- Feature: Real-time pain signal detection from public reviews and websites
- Benefit: Every call opens with something the prospect can independently verify
- Emotional: You show up informed, not desperate

---

## Brand Personality

**Archetype:** Magician (70%) + Sage (30%)
**2-word filter:** Effortless · Deliberate
**Voice:** The Knowing Advisor — knowing, wry, measured, authoritative
**Tone:** 50/100 casual-formal · 65/100 irreverent · 30/100 enthusiastic

---

## Messaging Tone
- Lead with the finding, not the feature
- No exclamation marks
- State positions without hedging
- Short sentences. Fragments permitted.
- End on the brand position, not a vague CTA
- **Never say:** leverage, seamless, AI-powered (as a claim), solution, optimise, scalable, game-changing

**Approved headline style:**
"You don't need to research them. We already did."
"Call with a receipt."
"One signal. One call. One reason."

---

## Channel Hints
- Twitter/X AI builder community — highest density of ICP
- Product Hunt launch — fits demo-theatre product well
- Indie Hackers — founder irony narrative resonates
- LinkedIn — secondary; ICP is there but less engaged
- TBD: paid channels, content strategy — fill during marketing-plan intake
